title: Backend: Node/Express greeting API + static file server
description:
Set up the Node.js + Express backend:
- GET /greet?name=<value> → JSON { greeting: "Hello, <name>!" }
- Falls back to "Hello, World!" when name is missing or empty
- Serves static files from public/ directory
- package.json with `npm start` script
- Listens on process.env.PORT || 3000

Repo: hello-world-1160af53-3802-40f7-a83f-ecd694175a27 (create if not exists, update project URL)

---

✅ COMPLETED

Implementation details:
- Created Express server in server.js with /greet endpoint
- Proper query parameter handling with trim() and fallback logic
- Static file serving configured for public/ directory
- package.json with express dependency and npm start script
- Created .gitignore for node_modules and environment files
- Added comprehensive README with setup and API documentation
- Created GitHub repository and pushed all files
- Updated project URL in Mission Control
- Verified functionality with local testing:
  - /greet → {"greeting":"Hello, World!"}
  - /greet?name=Alice → {"greeting":"Hello, Alice!"}
  - /greet?name= → {"greeting":"Hello, World!"}
  - Static file server serving index.html

Repository: https://github.com/ray-manguino-dev/hello-world-1160af53-3802-40f7-a83f-ecd694175a27
